recipes.title: Bacalhau à Brás
recipes.description: Shredded salt cod scrambled with crisp matchstick potatoes, onions and eggs, finished with olives and parsley.
steps: Soak salt cod 24 hours, changing water 3-4 times; drain, simmer 5 minutes, cool and flake.
Cut potatoes into thin matchsticks, rinse and pat dry.
Deep-fry potatoes in batches until golden and crisp; drain.
Sauté sliced onions and garlic in olive oil until soft.
Add flaked cod and stir 2 minutes until heated through.
Mix in fried potatoes, then pour over beaten eggs, stirring gently until just set.
Off heat, scatter olives and chopped parsley and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Caldo Verde
recipes.description: Portuguese kale and potato soup enriched with olive oil and slices of chouriço.
steps: Sauté chopped onion and garlic in olive oil until soft.
Add peeled chopped potatoes, water and salt; simmer 20 minutes until tender.
Slice kale into very thin ribbons.
Slice chouriço and pan-fry briefly until oils are released.
Blend the potato mixture until smooth and return to the pot.
Add kale and simmer 5 minutes until just tender.
Stir in extra olive oil, ladle into bowls and top with chouriço slices.

recipes.title: Pastel de Nata
recipes.description: Portuguese custard tarts with caramelised tops and shatteringly crisp puff pastry shells.
steps: Make a syrup: simmer sugar, water, lemon zest and cinnamon to 110°C; cool slightly.
Whisk flour with a little cold milk to a smooth paste, then warm rest of milk and whisk together; cook until thick.
Off heat, slowly whisk in syrup (discard cinnamon), then yolks; strain.
Roll puff pastry, then roll tightly into a log and cut into 12 discs.
Press discs into muffin tin, working pastry up the sides.
Pour custard into shells, filling 3/4 full.
Bake at 250°C (highest setting) 12-15 minutes until tops are blistered black.
Cool briefly and serve with cinnamon and icing sugar.

recipes.title: Bifana
recipes.description: Portuguese pork sandwich: thin pork cutlets simmered in garlicky white wine sauce, served on a roll.
steps: Slice pork into very thin cutlets and pound lightly.
Marinate in crushed garlic, wine, bay, paprika, piri piri and salt for 4 hours.
Heat lard in a wide pan and sear pork in batches; remove.
Pour marinade into pan and reduce by half.
Return pork to the sauce and simmer 5 minutes.
Stuff hot pork into split rolls and spoon over the sauce.

recipes.title: Cataplana
recipes.description: Algarve seafood stew of clams, prawns, white fish, chouriço and tomato cooked in a clamshell pan.
steps: Soak clams in salted water 30 minutes to purge sand.
In a wide pot, sweat sliced onions, garlic, pepper and chouriço in olive oil.
Add chopped tomatoes, bay and wine; simmer 10 minutes.
Layer fish chunks, prawns and clams over the base.
Cover tightly and steam 12 minutes until clams open and prawns curl.
Discard any closed clams, scatter coriander and serve straight from the pot.

recipes.title: Francesinha
recipes.description: Porto's iconic layered sandwich of steak, ham and sausage smothered in spicy beer-tomato sauce and melted cheese.
steps: Pan-fry steaks, sausages and linguiça until cooked; slice as needed.
Make sauce: melt butter, whisk in flour, then add beer, stock and passata.
Stir in piri piri and simmer 15 minutes until thick.
Layer ham, steak, linguiça and sausage between two slices of bread.
Place sandwich on a baking dish, top with cheese slices and grill until melted.
Pour the hot sauce over the sandwich and serve with chips.

recipes.title: Açorda
recipes.description: Portuguese bread soup flavoured with garlic, coriander and olive oil, finished with a poached egg.
steps: Pound garlic with salt and most of the coriander to a paste.
Stir paste with olive oil in a wide bowl.
Tear bread into chunks and add to the bowl.
Pour boiling water over and stir until bread softens to porridge consistency.
Poach eggs in simmering water until whites are set.
Ladle açorda into bowls, top each with a poached egg and remaining coriander.

recipes.title: Arroz de Pato
recipes.description: Portuguese duck rice baked with chouriço under a glossy crust.
steps: Simmer duck legs with onion, carrot, bay, wine and salt in 1.5L water for 75 minutes.
Remove duck, reserve stock; shred meat from bones.
Sauté finely chopped onion in olive oil; add rice and toast briefly.
Add 800ml of strained duck stock, season and simmer 12 minutes.
Stir in shredded duck and transfer to a baking dish.
Top with chouriço slices and bake at 200°C for 15 minutes until top is crisp.

recipes.title: Cozido à Portuguesa
recipes.description: Hearty Portuguese boiled dinner of mixed meats, sausages and vegetables.
steps: Place beef and pork in a large pot, cover with water and simmer 60 minutes; skim foam.
Add chicken and simmer 30 more minutes.
Add carrots, turnips and potatoes; cook 20 minutes.
Add chouriço, morcela and quartered cabbage; cook 15 minutes.
Cook rice separately using some of the broth.
Slice meats and arrange on a platter with vegetables, sausages and rice.

recipes.title: Carne de Porco à Alentejana
recipes.description: Alentejo dish of marinated pork cubes pan-fried with clams, served with crisp potatoes.
steps: Cube pork and marinate with garlic paste, wine, paprika, bay and salt for 4 hours.
Soak clams in salted water 30 minutes.
Cube potatoes and shallow-fry until golden; keep warm.
Remove pork from marinade and brown in olive oil.
Add reserved marinade and simmer 15 minutes until pork is tender.
Add clams, cover and cook 5 minutes until they open.
Stir in chopped coriander and serve over the fried potatoes.

recipes.title: Sardinhas Assadas
recipes.description: Charcoal-grilled fresh sardines, the heart of Portuguese summer festivals.
steps: Rinse sardines and pat dry; do not gut small ones.
Cover sardines generously with coarse salt and rest 1 hour.
Light charcoal and let it burn down to glowing embers.
Brush off excess salt and grill sardines 3 minutes per side until skin is blistered.
Serve immediately on slices of country bread with lemon and a drizzle of olive oil.

recipes.title: Pão de Ló
recipes.description: Portuguese sponge cake with a soft moist centre, made with little more than eggs and sugar.
steps: Whisk eggs and sugar over a pot of warm water until tripled in volume and very pale.
Off heat, whisk 5 more minutes until thick and ribbony.
Sift flour and zest over the foam and fold gently with a spatula.
Pour into a parchment-lined deep tin.
Bake at 180°C for 25 minutes; the centre should still wobble slightly.
Cool in the tin and dust with icing sugar before serving.

recipes.title: Polvo à Lagareiro
recipes.description: Roasted octopus drowned in olive oil and garlic, served with smashed potatoes.
steps: Simmer octopus with bay and an onion 50 minutes until tender; drain.
Boil potatoes whole in salted water until just tender; peel.
Place octopus and potatoes in a roasting tin and smash potatoes lightly with the heel of your hand.
Slice garlic and scatter over the top.
Pour olive oil generously over everything and season.
Roast at 200°C for 25 minutes until edges crisp and garlic is golden.
Sprinkle with parsley and serve with the pan oils spooned over.

recipes.title: Bacalhau à Gomes de Sá
recipes.description: Salt cod baked with potatoes, onions, olives and hard-boiled eggs from Porto.
steps: Soak cod 24 hours, changing water 3-4 times, then poach in milk + 300ml water 10 minutes; flake.
Boil potatoes in their skins until just tender, peel and slice thickly.
Hard-boil eggs, peel and quarter.
Sauté sliced onions and garlic with bay in olive oil until soft.
Layer potatoes, cod and onions in a baking dish.
Drizzle with olive oil and bake at 200°C for 25 minutes.
Top with olives, eggs and chopped parsley before serving.

recipes.title: Feijoada à Transmontana
recipes.description: Hearty northern Portuguese bean stew with pork, sausages and cabbage.
steps: Soak beans overnight; drain.
Simmer beans with bay in fresh water 60 minutes until almost tender.
In another pot, sauté chopped onions and garlic in olive oil.
Add pork, ribs, passata and cook 30 minutes with a little bean liquid.
Combine beans and meats with sliced carrots; simmer 30 minutes.
Add sliced sausages and shredded cabbage; cook 15 more minutes.
Serve with rice and crusty bread.

recipes.title: Pastéis de Bacalhau
recipes.description: Crisp Portuguese salt cod fritters bound with potato, onion and parsley.
steps: Soak cod 24 hours, changing water 3-4 times.
Simmer cod 10 minutes; cool, remove bones and skin and shred finely.
Boil potatoes whole; peel and mash dry.
Combine cod, mashed potato, finely chopped onion, garlic, parsley, eggs and pepper.
Knead until well combined; the texture should be firm but moist.
Heat oil to 180°C; shape mixture into quenelles using two spoons and drop into oil.
Fry until deeply golden, drain and serve hot.
